IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Swagger/AutoRest-based generation to TypeSpec-based generation.\n\nIt is a sub-issue of the parent tracking issue [#53864 – Azure Mgmt Generator migration](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864).\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Context \u0026 Background\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Service SDK**: `Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights` (located under `sdk/securityinsights/`)\n- **Migration Type**: MPG (Management Plane Generator) migration from AutoRest/Swagger → TypeSpec\n- **Parent tracker**: [#53864](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864) — tracks all Azure Mgmt Generator migrations\n- **Author**: Internal team member (`haiyuazhang`)\n- **Labels already Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Locate the TypeSpec spec for SecurityInsights in [azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/tree/main/specification/securityinsights)\n- [ ] Create or update `tsp-location.yaml` in the SDK package directory\n- [ ] Run `azsdk_package_generate_code` / `tsp-client update` to generate the SDK from TypeSpec\n- [ ] Build the generated SDK: `dotnet build`\n- [ ] Run tests: `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n- [ ] Check for breaking changes against current GA version (ApiCompat)\n- [ ] Add customizations if needed (`Customizations/` folder) for breaking change mitigations\n- [ ] Run `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 securityinsights` to update public API listings\n- [ ] Run pre-commit checks (dotnet format, snippets, etc.)\n- [ ] Open PR with the migration patterns](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mitigate-breaking-changes/SKILL.md)\n- [local-sdk-workflow migrations tracker #55080](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55080)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e⚠️ Common Migration Pitfalls\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Breaking changes**: Check for removed/renamed types, changed property types, or removed base types. Use `CodeGenSuppress`, `CodeGenType`, partial class customizations, or TypeSpec `@@clientName` / `@@access` decorators to mitigate.\n- **WirePathAttribute**: If the existing package uses `WirePathAttribute`, enable `enable-wire-path-attribute: true` in `tspconfig.yaml`.\n- **Flattened properties**: Ensure `FlattenPropertyVisitor` handles all edge cases (models with no public constructor).\n- **Sync method scope**: Verify that generated sync methods include `using DiagnosticScope scope = ...` (known generator issue in some collection/resource files).\n- **ModelFactory**: Watch for `@@clientName` on union types causing broken `global::.TypeName` references in `ModelFactory` generated code.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":56944},"name":"add_comment"}}}
